@graph.contextPackSummary: Malcolm drags himself along the barrier. His leg is bad —

@graph.synopsis: Grant and the kids hit the paddock floor running. Rain. Darkness.
@graph.scriptText:
61  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK - CONTINUOUS

    Grant and the kids hit the paddock floor running. Rain. Darkness.
    Ahead: thick jungle. Behind them: the T-Rex bellows from the
    top of the barrier, too high to follow.

    Grant runs with TIM gripping his hand, LEX a step behind. They
    plunge into the treeline.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant and the kids hit the paddock floor running. Rain. Darkness.

@graph.slugline: EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK - BASE OF BARRIER - NIGHT
@graph.synopsis: They stop in the shelter of the trees. Grant listens. The T-Rex
@graph.scriptText:
62  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK - BASE OF BARRIER - NIGHT

    They stop in the shelter of the trees. Grant listens. The T-Rex
    is on the road above — moving, her footfalls shaking the earth.

    Lex is crying silently. Tim is pale but focused. Grant crouches
    to their level.

                        GRANT
              (barely a whisper)
              Listen to me. Are you hurt?

    They shake their heads.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              Good. We're going to be okay. But I need you
              both to be very quiet. Can you do that?

    Tim nods. Lex nods.

    They hear the T-Rex above: still moving, still close.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They stop in the shelter of the trees. Grant listens. The T-Rex

@graph.synopsis: MALCOLM is down. He dragged himself to a cluster of large
@graph.scriptText:
63  EXT. PARK ROAD - CONTINUOUS

    MALCOLM is down. He dragged himself to a cluster of large
    boulders near the road's edge and collapsed. His leg is bad.
    He's alive, barely.

    The T-Rex moves along the road above. Her feet shake the ground
    with each step.

    Malcolm, flat on his back, watches her move past.

                        MALCOLM
              (barely breathing)
              Life... finds a way.

    He almost passes out. He doesn't.
@graph.contextPackSummary: MALCOLM is down. He dragged himself to a cluster of large

@graph.synopsis: The T-Rex finds the PORTA-POTTY shelter. She lowers her head.
@graph.scriptText:
64  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK - ROAD - NIGHT

    The T-Rex finds the PORTA-POTTY shelter. She lowers her head.
    Sniffs.

    She headbutts it. The whole structure shatters. And there —
    cowering on the toilet, his trousers pooled at his feet —

    GENNARO.

    He looks up at the T-Rex.

                        GENNARO
              Ni — nice girl. Easy girl. Easy —

    She eats him. Not entirely on screen, but the audience gets
    the idea. There's a tremendous crash of wood and a brief
    terrible sound.

    Then: silence except for rain.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The T-Rex finds the PORTA-POTTY shelter. She lowers her head.

@graph.synopsis: The T-Rex lifts her head. She's noticed something. She turns.
@graph.scriptText:
65  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK - ROAD - CONTINUOUS

    The T-Rex lifts her head. She's noticed something. She turns.
    She SEES car one — which went over the barrier and is now wedged
    in the trees at the base of the barrier wall.

    She examines it with interest. She pushes it with her snout.

    Inside the wrecked car: TIM. He's alive. He hid in the car.

    He presses himself flat to the seat. He is trying not to
    breathe.

    The T-Rex bites the car. The car shudders.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The T-Rex lifts her head. She's noticed something. She turns.

@graph.slugline: EXT. BASE OF BARRIER WALL - CONTINUOUS
@graph.synopsis: Grant sees the car from the trees. He sees Tim inside.
@graph.scriptText:
66  EXT. BASE OF BARRIER WALL - CONTINUOUS

    Grant sees the car from the trees. He sees Tim inside.

                        GRANT
              (to Lex)
              Don't move. Don't move at all. I mean it.
              I'll be back.

    He steps out of the treeline.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant sees the car from the trees. He sees Tim inside.

@graph.slugline: EXT. BASE OF BARRIER WALL - CONTINUOUS
@graph.synopsis: Grant moves toward the car. He grabs a thick branch and breaks
@graph.scriptText:
67  EXT. BASE OF BARRIER WALL - CONTINUOUS

    Grant moves toward the car. He grabs a thick branch and breaks
    it — an audible crack. The T-Rex's head swings toward the sound.

    Grant is in the open. He holds absolutely still.

    The T-Rex stares at him. He stares back.

    A second. Two. Five.

    She looks away, back at the car. She pushes the car again. The
    car rocks, tilts, begins to slide down the slope at the base
    of the barrier wall.

                        GRANT
              (calling to Tim)
              Tim! Tim can you hear me?
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant moves toward the car. He grabs a thick branch and breaks

@graph.synopsis: Tim can hear him. The car is upside down, resting at an angle.
@graph.scriptText:
68  INT. WRECKED CAR - CONTINUOUS

    Tim can hear him. The car is upside down, resting at an angle.
    Tim is braced against the ceiling.

                        GRANT (OUTSIDE, MUFFLED)
              Tim! I'm here! Stay with me!

    The T-Rex shoves the car again. Tim tumbles. The car slides
    further.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Tim can hear him. The car is upside down, resting at an angle.

@graph.synopsis: The car slides down the slope and catches in the branches of
@graph.scriptText:
69  EXT. BASE OF BARRIER - CONTINUOUS

    The car slides down the slope and catches in the branches of
    a large tree growing at the base of the barrier. It's balanced
    precariously — jammed in the crook of the tree, about fifteen
    feet above the ground.

    The T-Rex loses interest. She sniffs around, then turns and moves
    back toward the road.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The car slides down the slope and catches in the branches of

@graph.synopsis: Grant watches the T-Rex go. He waits. She doesn't come back.
@graph.scriptText:
70  EXT. BASE OF BARRIER - CONTINUOUS

    Grant watches the T-Rex go. He waits. She doesn't come back.

    He looks up at the wrecked car, jammed in the tree.

                        GRANT
              Tim!

                        TIM (FROM THE CAR)
              I'm okay! I think I'm okay!

                        GRANT
              Can you move?

                        TIM
              I think I'm upside down.

                        GRANT
              Can you get to the window?

    A pause. Sounds of movement.

                        TIM
              Yes. I can see you.

                        GRANT
              Good. Stay there. I'm coming up.

    He looks around. Finds footholds in the barrier wall — it's not
    smooth concrete, there are seams and ledges where the drainage
    pipes run. He begins to climb.

    LEX has crept out of the trees. She watches from below.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant watches the T-Rex go. He waits. She doesn't come back.

@graph.synopsis: Grant reaches the car. The door is partially open. He gets it
@graph.scriptText:
71  EXT. TREE - NIGHT

    Grant reaches the car. The door is partially open. He gets it
    open wider.

    TIM is there — bruised, wet, unsteady, but alive. He looks at
    Grant with enormous relief.

                        TIM
              Dr. Grant.

                        GRANT
              I've got you.

    He helps Tim out of the wrecked car. Getting him out is delicate —
    the car shifts as weight changes. Grant moves carefully, keeping
    Tim close, moving along a branch toward the trunk.

    Below them: LEX watches, hands over her mouth.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant reaches the car. The door is partially open. He gets it

@graph.synopsis: The car shifts. GRANT clutches the branch. TIM clutches Grant.
@graph.scriptText:
72  EXT. TREE - CONTINUOUS

    The car shifts. GRANT clutches the branch. TIM clutches Grant.

    The car slips and FALLS — crashing through the branches below,
    impact shaking the whole tree.

    They hold on.

    The car lands somewhere below. A terrible crash.

    Silence.

                        TIM
              (eyes closed, gripping Grant)
              Is that... it?

                        GRANT
              That's it.

    They stay there for a moment, both catching their breath.

                        TIM
              Dr. Grant?

                        GRANT
              Yeah.

                        TIM
              I prefer the vehicles.

    Despite everything, Grant almost laughs.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The car shifts. GRANT clutches the branch. TIM clutches Grant.

@graph.synopsis: They climb down from the tree. Grant, then Tim, lowered the
@graph.scriptText:
73  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK - NIGHT

    They climb down from the tree. Grant, then Tim, lowered the
    last few feet. They reunite with Lex at the bottom.

    Lex grabs Tim and hugs him. Tim lets her.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They climb down from the tree. Grant, then Tim, lowered the

@graph.synopsis: Grant surveys the scene. Road flares smoldering in the rain.
@graph.scriptText:
74  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK - ROAD - NIGHT

    Grant surveys the scene. Road flares smoldering in the rain.
    Wrecked car somewhere below. No sign of Malcolm or Gennaro.

    He looks at the fence line. They need to move — the T-Rex
    could come back.

                        GRANT
              We have to find shelter. And we have to find
              the others. Stay close to me. Stay together.

    He starts moving along the fence line. The kids follow.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant surveys the scene. Road flares smoldering in the rain.

@graph.synopsis: They move through the dark. The rain has eased slightly. Grant
@graph.scriptText:
75  EXT. PARK - NIGHT

    They move through the dark. The rain has eased slightly. Grant
    leads, the kids close behind.

    In the darkness: sounds. Jungle sounds. Animal sounds.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They move through the dark. The rain has eased slightly. Grant

@graph.synopsis: Arnold is working fast, typing, trying to break through Nedry's
@graph.scriptText:
76  INT. CONTROL ROOM - NIGHT

    Arnold is working fast, typing, trying to break through Nedry's
    code block. Hammond paces behind him.

                        HAMMOND
              How long?

                        ARNOLD
              I don't know. His code is hidden inside a
              system patch. I need to find it and —

                        HAMMOND
              Where the hell is Nedry?

    Arnold doesn't answer. He's focused.

    Hammond slams his hand on a console.

                        HAMMOND (CONT'D)
              I will kill Nedry! I will personally kill
              that fat, disloyal —

                        MULDOON
              (very calm)
              We need to focus.

    He points at the storm display.

                        MULDOON (CONT'D)
              The tour vehicles are stopped at the T-Rex
              paddock. Contact is down. I need authorization
              to go in.

                        HAMMOND
              (pulling himself together)
              Yes. Yes. Go.

                        MULDOON
              I'll need Ellie.

                        HAMMOND
              Take whoever you need.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Arnold is working fast, typing, trying to break through Nedry's

@graph.synopsis: Arnold continues working. He navigates the code.
@graph.scriptText:
77  INT. CONTROL ROOM - NIGHT - CONTINUOUS

    Arnold continues working. He navigates the code.

                        ARNOLD
              It's a full system shutdown and restart.
              Nedry coded it to wipe clean when I try
              to override. I'll need to —

    He stares at the screen.

                        ARNOLD (CONT'D)
              I can find it. But without Nedry's password,
              the only way to undo this is a complete
              system shutdown. Wipe everything. Reboot
              from scratch.

                        HAMMOND
              Then do that.

                        ARNOLD
              If I do that, everything goes off. Every
              system. The fences, the doors, the phones,
              everything. It'll take fifteen minutes for
              the generators to reboot.

                        HAMMOND
              And then it comes back on?

                        ARNOLD
              If the main system recognizes the restart.
              If.

    A pause.

                        HAMMOND
              Do it.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Arnold continues working. He navigates the code.

@graph.synopsis: Nedry drives Jeep 12 in pouring rain. He's sweating, leaning
@graph.scriptText:
78  EXT. VISITOR'S CENTER ROAD - NIGHT

    Nedry drives Jeep 12 in pouring rain. He's sweating, leaning
    forward over the wheel, trying to navigate. The road is
    impossible in this weather.

    He checks a hand-drawn MAP on the seat beside him. He peers
    through the windshield.

    He's lost.

                        NEDRY
              Where is it? This doesn't — the road should
              be right HERE —

    He slows. He stops. He gets out — into the driving rain — to
    look at the map.

    He's in the middle of the jungle. The road has split and he
    took the wrong fork.

    He gets back in the Jeep, puts it in reverse, and immediately
    drives the rear wheel into a ditch.

                        NEDRY (CONT'D)
              Oh, great. Great! GREAT!

    He guns it. The wheel spins in the mud. He guns it again.
    He gets out again.

    He opens the trunk and gets the CABLE AND HOOK — a tow cable.
    He'll have to hook it to a tree.

    He slings it over his shoulder and trudges into the jungle
    toward the nearest tree.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Nedry drives Jeep 12 in pouring rain. He's sweating, leaning

@graph.synopsis: Dark. Dense. Nedry fumbles through the underbrush, cable
@graph.scriptText:
79  EXT. JUNGLE - CONTINUOUS

    Dark. Dense. Nedry fumbles through the underbrush, cable
    over his shoulder, the Barbasol can clutched to his chest.

    Then — a SOUND. Something in the brush nearby. A rustle.
    Then silence.

    Nedry stops.

    From the darkness: a DILOPHOSAURUS appears. It's smaller than
    the big predators — waist height on a person — with a bright
    frill around its neck that flares suddenly in a pattern of
    alarming color. Its eyes: intelligent, curious.

    Nedry stares at it.

                        NEDRY
              Nice. Nice — nice girl. Nice boy. Whichever.
              I don't even know. Are you the one that
              spits?

    The dilophosaurus tilts its head. Its frill flares again.
    It makes a sound — a hooting, rattling call.

                        NEDRY (CONT'D)
              That's okay. I'll just — you stay here.
              You stay right there. Good.

    He backs up slowly. The dilophosaurus follows, curious, head
    tilted the other way now.

                        NEDRY (CONT'D)
              No, no, stay. Staaaay.

    He's almost to the Jeep. He reaches back, gets his hand on
    the door handle —

    The dilophosaurus SPITS. A gobbet of dark venom hits Nedry
    squarely in the face. He screams — recoils — grabs at his
    eyes.

                        NEDRY (CONT'D)
              MY EYES. I CAN'T — I —

    He stumbles, knocks into the Jeep door. The Barbasol can
    falls. He grabs for it, blind, finds it, clutches it.

    The dilophosaurus is right in front of him now.

    She hoots softly.

    Nedry, unable to see, fumbles for the Jeep door. He gets it
    open. Gets halfway in.

    The dilophosaurus lunges.

    The Jeep door swings shut. The Barbasol can falls in the mud.
    The rain falls on it.

    The jungle is quiet.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Dark. Dense. Nedry fumbles through the underbrush, cable

@graph.synopsis: Muldoon has gone. Hammond is in the control room with Arnold.
@graph.scriptText:
80  INT. CONTROL ROOM - NIGHT

    Muldoon has gone. Hammond is in the control room with Arnold.
    Arnold is preparing to initiate the shutdown.

                        ARNOLD
              When I hit this, everything goes. Fifteen
              minutes minimum before I can start bringing
              systems back up one at a time.

                        HAMMOND
              Do it.

    Arnold takes a breath. He types the shutdown command.

    Everything goes dark.

    Every screen in the control room. Every monitor. The room is
    lit only by emergency lighting — red, dim.

    Hammond stands in the red light, surrounded by dark screens.

                        HAMMOND (CONT'D)
              (quietly)
              Arnold. When the systems come back up.

                        ARNOLD
              Yeah?

                        HAMMOND
              They do come back up?

    A pause.

                        ARNOLD
              Usually.


A80 EXT. PARK - NIGHT

    Muldoon and ELLIE move through the park in a park jeep. Muldoon
    drives, headlights cutting through the rain. He has a shotgun
    across his knees.

                        ELLIE
              Grant's out there.

                        MULDOON
              I know.

                        ELLIE
              And the kids.

                        MULDOON
              I know, Dr. Sattler.

    He drives.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Muldoon has gone. Hammond is in the control room with Arnold.

@graph.synopsis: Muldoon's jeep arrives at the T-Rex paddock. The headlights
@graph.scriptText:
81  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK ROAD - NIGHT

    Muldoon's jeep arrives at the T-Rex paddock. The headlights
    show: wrecked tour car two, on its side. Tour car one: gone.
    Debris. Scattered emergency supplies.

    Muldoon stops the jeep. They get out.

    He sweeps a flashlight beam around. No people.

                        ELLIE
              Alan! Tim! Lex!

    Her voice disappears into the rain. No answer.

                        MULDOON
              (low)
              Don't shout.

    He's looking at the paddock fence. It's down. Dark. Unelectric.

                        MULDOON (CONT'D)
              She could still be in the area.

    He scans the darkness.

                        ELLIE
              (tight)
              Where are they?

    Muldoon moves along the road, flashlight sweeping. He finds
    the spot where the porta-potty was. Just splinters.

    He says nothing. Just keeps moving.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Muldoon's jeep arrives at the T-Rex paddock. The headlights

@graph.synopsis: The flashlight finds MALCOLM. He's propped against the barrier
@graph.scriptText:
82  EXT. TYRANNOSAUR PADDOCK ROAD - CONTINUOUS

    The flashlight finds MALCOLM. He's propped against the barrier
    wall, in and out of consciousness. His leg is badly injured.

    Ellie drops to her knees beside him.

                        ELLIE
              Ian! Ian, look at me.

                        MALCOLM
              (eyes opening, unfocused)
              Should've... stayed in chaos theory...

                        ELLIE
              (to Muldoon)
              He's hurt. His leg —

                        MULDOON
              I see it.

    He moves to help.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The flashlight finds MALCOLM. He's propped against the barrier

@graph.synopsis: Grant leads the kids through the park. They've been walking
@graph.scriptText:
83  EXT. PARK GROUNDS - NIGHT

    Grant leads the kids through the park. They've been walking
    for a while. The rain is easing but the dark is total.

    Lex is beginning to falter.

                        LEX
              I can't keep going.

                        GRANT
              Yes you can.

                        LEX
              I'm tired and I'm cold and I want to go
              back.

                        GRANT
              (quietly)
              There's nothing to go back to right now.
              Okay? We're going to find shelter, get
              warm, and wait for help.

                        LEX
              Are they even looking for us?

    Tim puts a hand on Lex's shoulder. She shrugs him off, but
    she keeps walking.

    Ahead: the base of a large embankment. Trees. The beginning
    of a slope.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant leads the kids through the park. They've been walking

@graph.synopsis: A massive tree. Old, with wide spreading branches. Grant looks
@graph.scriptText:
84  EXT. TREE - NIGHT

    A massive tree. Old, with wide spreading branches. Grant looks
    up at it.

                        GRANT
              Can you climb?

                        TIM
              Yes.

                        LEX
              No.

                        TIM
              She can. She's lying.

    Lex glares at him.

                        GRANT
              Up we go. It's safer up high.

    He helps Lex to the first branch. Tim goes up himself, quick
    and sure. Grant follows.

    They settle into the crook of the tree — high enough that
    nothing can easily reach them, sheltered by the canopy.

    Grant looks out through the branches at the dark park.

    Tim looks at him.

                        TIM
              Dr Grant?

                        GRANT
              Yeah.

                        TIM
              Do you hate kids?

    A pause.

                        GRANT
              I don't hate kids.

                        TIM
              You don't like them.

    Another pause.

                        GRANT
              ...I'm not completely comfortable with them.

                        TIM
              Because they're not fossils.

    Grant looks at him.

                        GRANT
              ...That's... very perceptive.

                        TIM
              My dad said you were too smart to have
              kids. Mom said you were too scared.

                        GRANT
              (taking this in)
              Your mom's a wise woman.

    He puts an arm around Tim. Tim doesn't object.

    Lex, on the other side, watches this. She carefully, slowly,
    leans in and puts her head against Grant's other shoulder.

    He sits there in the tree, in the dark, with two children
    he barely knows and barely knows what to do with.

    He doesn't move.
@graph.contextPackSummary: A massive tree. Old, with wide spreading branches. Grant looks

@graph.synopsis: Earlier. Shortly after the T-Rex attack. LEX had hidden in
@graph.scriptText:
85  EXT. CULVERT - NIGHT

    Earlier. Shortly after the T-Rex attack. LEX had hidden in
    a concrete drainage culvert while Grant climbed for Tim.
    The culvert is cold, damp.

    She's curled up in the entrance. She's not moving.

    Above the culvert: the T-Rex's footfalls. She can feel them
    in the concrete.

    The footfalls recede.

    She waits.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Earlier. Shortly after the T-Rex attack. LEX had hidden in

@graph.synopsis: Muldoon's jeep. MALCOLM is in the back seat, leg improvised-
@graph.scriptText:
86  EXT. PARK ROAD - NIGHT

    Muldoon's jeep. MALCOLM is in the back seat, leg improvised-
    splinted with bits of a headrest support. He's pale. He's
    talking anyway.

                        MALCOLM
              The, ah... the key issue with the system's
              behavior at the edge of...

                        ELLIE
              Ian. Shh.

                        MALCOLM
              I was only going to say that...

                        ELLIE
              Ian.

    He closes his eyes.

    Muldoon drives. He slows as they approach a section of road.

                        MULDOON
              Hold on.

    He stops. Gets out. He sweeps his flashlight across the road.
    Footprints in the mud. Large ones.

    He looks into the darkness on either side.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Muldoon's jeep. MALCOLM is in the back seat, leg improvised-

@graph.synopsis: Muldoon finds the wrecked tour car one — it went over the
@graph.scriptText:
87  EXT. CLEARING - NIGHT

    Muldoon finds the wrecked tour car one — it went over the
    barrier and is lying on its side in the paddock clearing.
    He examines it carefully. Empty. Doors open.

    Grant's footprints leading away from the car — and two sets
    of smaller prints. Kids' shoes.

                        MULDOON
              (to himself)
              They made it.

    He marks the direction. Back to the jeep.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Muldoon finds the wrecked tour car one — it went over the

@graph.synopsis: Muldoon is driving back along the paddock road. He stops.
@graph.scriptText:
88  EXT. PARK ROAD - NIGHT

    Muldoon is driving back along the paddock road. He stops.

    Out the windshield: a shape in the headlights.

    The T-Rex. On the road. Looking at the jeep.

                        MULDOON
              (very quietly)
              Don't move.

    Ellie goes still. Malcolm, in the back, is barely conscious
    but feels the jeep stop.

                        MALCOLM
              (eyes closed)
              Is it... the rex?

    Nobody answers.

    The T-Rex looks at the jeep. Her head lowers. She's evaluating.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Muldoon is driving back along the paddock road. He stops.

@graph.synopsis: The T-Rex takes a step toward the jeep. Then another.
@graph.scriptText:
89  EXT. PARK ROAD - CONTINUOUS

    The T-Rex takes a step toward the jeep. Then another.

    Muldoon puts the jeep in reverse — slowly — and begins to
    back up.

    The T-Rex follows. She's picking up speed.

                        MULDOON
              Hold on.

    He floors it — in reverse. The jeep rockets backward down the
    road. The T-Rex RUNS after them, impossibly fast, her strides
    enormous.

    Ellie screams.

    Malcolm grabs the seat in front of him.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The T-Rex takes a step toward the jeep. Then another.

@graph.synopsis: The jeep tears backward through the rain, T-Rex in pursuit,
@graph.scriptText:
90  EXT. PARK ROAD - CONTINUOUS

    The jeep tears backward through the rain, T-Rex in pursuit,
    gaining.

    Muldoon spins the wheel — the jeep does a reverse skid, comes
    around a bend in the road, plunges into the treeline on a
    side path.

    The T-Rex overshoots the bend. Her momentum carries her past.
    She scrabbles on the wet road, turns, searches.

    The jeep is gone. Into the dark jungle.

    The T-Rex stands in the road, rain pouring off her scales.
    She bellows — frustration, fury — into the dark.

    Then she turns and walks away.

    In the jeep: everyone breathes.

                        MALCOLM
              (from the back seat, eyes still closed)
              I hate this park.

===============================================================================
END OF ACT TWO-B — Scene 90
Next: act3_scenes_91-141.txt
===============================================================================
@graph.contextPackSummary: The jeep tears backward through the rain, T-Rex in pursuit,

@graph.synopsis: Grant, Tim, and Lex have descended from the tree. Gray pre-dawn
@graph.scriptText:
91  EXT. PARK GROUND - NIGHT

    Grant, Tim, and Lex have descended from the tree. Gray pre-dawn
    light is beginning to filter through the canopy. They've been
    moving for an hour.

    Ahead: open grassland. Broad and flat.

    They step out of the trees —

    And STOP.

    The grassland is FULL of movement. A HERD of GALLIMIMUS —
    dozens of them, fast, graceful, birdlike — running in a mass
    across the open ground. Their feet drum against the earth.
    The sound builds to a thundering.

                        GRANT
              (quiet; awed)
              Gallimimus.

    Tim stares. His mouth is open.

    They're passing thirty feet away. Close enough to feel the
    wind of their passage. Grant puts his arms out to keep the
    kids back — not from danger, exactly, but from instinct.

    The herd flows around the edge of a tree cluster — and then
    something changes. The herd shifts, bunches, turns. An
    involuntary mass movement.

    Grant sees why.

    The T-REX appears from the far end of the field, running full
    bore, in full stride. She's among them. The herd panics.

                        TIM
              (pulling Grant's sleeve)
              Run! Run!

                        GRANT
              Stay absolutely still.

    They freeze. The herd streams past on both sides of them,
    close — breath and scale and the thunder of feet.

    The T-Rex takes a gallimimus in mid-stride. Snaps it up.

    The herd passes. The thunder fades.

    The T-Rex stands in the field, head down, feeding.

    Grant watches her.

                        TIM
              (very quietly)
              She's a... she's a killer.

                        GRANT
              (equally quiet)
              She's not a killer. She's just... hungry.
              Like every other animal. She's magnificent.

    He says it with complete conviction.

    Tim looks at him. Then looks back at the T-Rex. He's trying
    to see what Grant sees.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              Come on. Move.

    They slip back into the trees.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant, Tim, and Lex have descended from the tree. Gray pre-dawn

@graph.synopsis: Earlier. The tree. Grant and the two kids, settled in the
@graph.scriptText:
92  EXT. TREE - NIGHT

    Earlier. The tree. Grant and the two kids, settled in the
    branches. Tim has fallen asleep against Grant's arm.

    LEX is awake. She studies Grant.

                        LEX
              (whispering)
              Do you have a family?

                        GRANT
              (whispering)
              No.

                        LEX
              Do you want one?

    A pause.

                        GRANT
              ...I don't know.

                        LEX
              Ellie wants to have babies.

                        GRANT
              I know.

                        LEX
              She said she doesn't want to wait for you
              to figure out if you want them too.

    Grant looks at the kid. The kid looks back.

                        GRANT
              She said that?

                        LEX
              She said something like that.

    Grant is quiet for a while.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Earlier. The tree. Grant and the two kids, settled in the

@graph.synopsis: Tim sleeping. Lex sleeping. Grant awake, watching the park.
@graph.scriptText:
93  EXT. TREE - LATER THAT NIGHT

    Tim sleeping. Lex sleeping. Grant awake, watching the park.

    He sees something in the dark below. A shape moves slowly
    through the undergrowth. A small, elegant herbivore — peaceful.
    It doesn't know he's there.

    Grant watches it, completely still, completely rapt.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Tim sleeping. Lex sleeping. Grant awake, watching the park.

@graph.synopsis: Meanwhile. The restaurant is dark. Most of the food has gone
@graph.scriptText:
94  INT. VISITOR'S CENTER RESTAURANT - NIGHT

    Meanwhile. The restaurant is dark. Most of the food has gone
    bad in the power outage. HAMMOND sits alone at a table with
    a pint of MELTING ICE CREAM. The only light is from an
    emergency lamp.

    He's eating. Methodically. He's on his third bowl.

    ELLIE finds him here. She sits across from him. She doesn't say
    anything for a moment.

                        HAMMOND
              I'm finished with this one.

    He pushes a bowl toward her.

                        HAMMOND (CONT'D)
              Didn't have anybody eat the mint with chip.

    She eats a spoonful. It's almost completely melted.

                        HAMMOND (CONT'D)
              You know, I had a flea circus when I was
              young. Never spent more than a few shillings
              on a set-up. But the people believed it.
              The people believed there were real fleas
              doing things. They weren't, of course. But
              the act of belief... the act of imagination...

    He looks at his spoon.

                        HAMMOND (CONT'D)
              I was wrong about this park. Was I wrong
              about everything?

    Ellie looks at him.

                        ELLIE
              John. You have these animals, and you built
              this park, and now you're trying to control
              whether they live or die. You can't.

                        HAMMOND
              People control animals all the time.
              We do it everywhere.

                        ELLIE
              Not when the animals are this. These are
              living things, and they will find a way to
              assert their nature. Ian was right.

    Hammond nods slowly. He eats another spoonful of melted
    ice cream.

                        HAMMOND
              Spared no expense.

    He says it softly. To himself. It sounds different than it
    used to.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Meanwhile. The restaurant is dark. Most of the food has gone

@graph.synopsis: Grant and the kids moving. Dawn light. Wet ground. Birds calling.
@graph.scriptText:
95  EXT. PARK - DAWN - GROUND LEVEL

    Grant and the kids moving. Dawn light. Wet ground. Birds calling.

    They emerge at the edge of what seems to be an open paddock
    area. Grant checks his bearings. He has a PARK MAP from the
    tour car — crumpled, slightly damp, but legible.

                        GRANT
              The perimeter fence should be... that way.
              About a mile.

                        TIM
              Is the fence on?

                        GRANT
              I don't know.

    He looks at the map. Then at the sky.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              Come on.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant and the kids moving. Dawn light. Wet ground. Birds calling.

@graph.synopsis: The light is beautiful now — low and golden. Birds everywhere.
@graph.scriptText:
96  EXT. PARK - DAWN

    The light is beautiful now — low and golden. Birds everywhere.
    The park, in this light, looks almost safe.

    They cross an open area. The ground is soft and wet from
    the storm.

    They reach an enormous FIG TREE at the edge of the clearing.
    Its roots fan out like a cathedral arch.

    Tim looks up into the branches.

                        TIM
              Can we rest? My feet hurt.

    Grant looks at Lex. She looks at him.

                        GRANT
              Ten minutes.

    He sits at the base of the tree. The kids sit next to him.

    A moment of almost-peace.

    Then — from the branches above — a SOUND. A deep, resonant
    thrumming. Warm.

    A BRACHIOSAURUS head appears above the tree line, drifting
    down toward them with improbable gentleness.

    Tim opens his eyes and freezes.

    The brachiosaurus brings her head all the way down — eyes wide,
    curious — and begins to eat from the top of the fig tree.
    She's close enough to touch.

    Lex stares. Her hand moves slowly out toward the dinosaur's
    cheek.

                        LEX
              (barely a breath)
              She's... soft.

    The brachiosaurus turns her eye toward Lex. A huge, gentle
    eye. She blinks.

    Lex touches her. Gently.

    Grant watches the two of them.


97  ON THE GROUND - DAWN

    Grant is moving through a section of undergrowth nearby,
    stretching his legs while the kids rest with the brachiosaurus.

    He stops.

    On the ground: an EGG SHELL. Fresh. And near it — another.
    And near that — a shallow scrape in the earth that looks
    very much like a nest.

    He crouches. He looks at the shells carefully.

    He knows what he's looking at.

    He calls back, low.

                        GRANT
              Tim. Come here. Quietly.

    Tim pads over. Grant shows him the shells.

                        TIM
              Raptor eggs?

                        GRANT
              (very quiet, very serious)
              They're not supposed to be able to breed.
              They're all female.

                        TIM
              Malcolm said something about frog DNA —

                        GRANT
              West African tree frog. They can change sex
              in a single-sex environment.

    He stands. He looks around.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              They're breeding.

    He stands very still.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              (more to himself)
              They're out here. Breeding. Beyond the
              perimeter.

    He makes a decision.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              We need to get to the perimeter fence. Now.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The light is beautiful now — low and golden. Birds everywhere.

@graph.synopsis: Arnold is at his console. The system has rebooted and he's
@graph.scriptText:
98  INT. CONTROL ROOM - DAY

    Arnold is at his console. The system has rebooted and he's
    been going through the restart sequence. HAMMOND paces.
    MULDOON and ELLIE are in the room. MALCOLM is on a
    cot nearby, leg braced, in obvious pain.

                        ARNOLD
              I can get everything back. Most things.
              But I need to physically flip the switch
              in the maintenance shed. The computer
              restart won't engage the park's main
              power grid without a manual reset at
              the breaker point.

                        HAMMOND
              So go flip the switch.

                        ARNOLD
              The raptor paddock is... there are indications
              the fence on the east side was impacted
              during the power loss.

                        MULDOON
              How impacted?

                        ARNOLD
              (looking at a printout)
              Could be the fence is down on the east side.

    A silence.

                        MULDOON
              If the fence went down, the raptors —

                        ARNOLD
              May have gotten out. Yes.

    Another silence.

                        MALCOLM
              (from the cot)
              The lysine contingency.

                        HAMMOND
              No.

                        MALCOLM
              If the raptors are loose —

                        HAMMOND
              The lysine contingency is not on the table.

                        MALCOLM
              Just because you don't want to hear it —

                        HAMMOND
              These are living things, Ian! They are not
              products to be simply switched off because
              they're inconvenient! I will not hear it!

    A pause.

                        MALCOLM
              (quieter)
              Hammond. Six people are dead.

    The room is quiet.

                        ARNOLD
              I'll go to the shed. It's the only option.

    He stubs out his cigarette.

                        ARNOLD (CONT'D)
              I'll call you on the radio when I get there.


A99 EXT. COMPOUND - DAY

    Arnold moves across the compound toward the maintenance shed.
    His path is exposed — open ground between the main building
    and the shed. He moves fast, head down.

    He doesn't look at the fence line to his right.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Arnold is at his console. The system has rebooted and he's

@graph.synopsis: In the park. Near the perimeter fence.
@graph.scriptText:
96D EXT. PARK GROUNDS - DAY

    In the park. Near the perimeter fence.

    GRANT has found a JEEP track — a maintenance road running
    along the inside of the perimeter fence. He flags the direction.

                        GRANT
              The fence runs this way to the main
              compound. We follow it. We don't touch it.

                        TIM
              Is it on?

                        GRANT
              I still don't know.

    He tears off a piece of dried grass and holds it near the
    fence wire.

    Nothing happens.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              Off. For now. But it could come back on
              at any time.

    He looks at the fence. It's tall. Electrified when active.
    A chain-link design with insulating stanchions.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              We're going to have to climb it.

    Tim studies it.

                        TIM
              How do we know when it's safe?

                        GRANT
              We don't.
@graph.contextPackSummary: In the park. Near the perimeter fence.

@graph.synopsis: They approach the fence. Grant tests the wire with a stick.
@graph.scriptText:
99  EXT. FENCE - DAY

    They approach the fence. Grant tests the wire with a stick.
    No spark.

                        GRANT
              Alright. I'll go first.

    He starts to climb. The fence sways with his weight. He goes
    carefully, methodically, hand over hand.

    At the top: a roll of concertina wire. He works around it.

    He drops down the other side.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              Okay. Lex, you next.

    Lex starts up. She's slower than Grant but determined.
    Halfway up, she stops.

                        LEX
              I can't.

                        GRANT
              You can.

                        LEX
              I'm slipping.

                        GRANT
              I know. Your left hand, move it up.

    She moves it up. Grips. Keeps climbing.

    She clears the concertina wire at the top — Grant guides
    her hands. She gets over. She drops. Grant catches her.

    TIM starts up.

    He's good at this. He's fast. He reaches the top —
@graph.contextPackSummary: They approach the fence. Grant tests the wire with a stick.

@graph.synopsis: Ellie, Muldoon, Hammond, and Malcolm wait in the underground
@graph.scriptText:
100 INT. BUNKER - DAY

    Ellie, Muldoon, Hammond, and Malcolm wait in the underground
    bunker. The room is spare, lit by emergency battery lights.
    Malcolm is propped against the wall.

    A radio. They wait for Arnold's voice on it.

    Nothing.

                        ELLIE
              He should have checked in by now.

                        MULDOON
              Give him time.

    More silence.

                        HAMMOND
              (to the radio)
              Ray? Ray, come in.

    Static.

                        ELLIE
              How long do we wait?

                        MULDOON
              (reaching for his weapon)
              We don't.

                        ELLIE
              I'll go.

    Hammond looks at her.

                        HAMMOND
              Absolutely not. You're —

                        ELLIE
              (standing up)
              If you tell me I'm a woman and I shouldn't
              go, I will hurt you. And I have access to
              sharp things.

    Hammond blinks.

                        ELLIE (CONT'D)
              Muldoon, come with me.

                        MULDOON
              (to Hammond)
              She's right.

    He stands. Checks the SPAS-12 shotgun he's been carrying.

    Malcolm opens his eyes.

                        MALCOLM
              Ellie.

    She pauses.

                        MALCOLM (CONT'D)
              Be careful.

    She nods. They go.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Ellie, Muldoon, Hammond, and Malcolm wait in the underground

@graph.synopsis: Back at the fence. TIM is at the top. He starts to come over —
@graph.scriptText:
101 EXT. FENCE - DAY

    Back at the fence. TIM is at the top. He starts to come over —

    A HUMMING. Low at first. Then louder.

    Grant looks at his hands. The fence wire is humming.

                        GRANT
              Tim! The fence is coming back on! NOW!
              TIM, LET GO! GET OFF THE FENCE!

    He sees Tim fumbling at the top, trying to get over faster —

    The fence COMES ALIVE with power. TIM is thrown — a horrible
    snap and flash — he convulses on the fence and then falls,
    landing hard on the ground inside the perimeter.

    He's still.

    Grant is already climbing back over.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Back at the fence. TIM is at the top. He starts to come over —

@graph.synopsis: Grant lands and runs to Tim. He's on the ground, pale, not
@graph.scriptText:
102 EXT. FENCE - CONTINUOUS

    Grant lands and runs to Tim. He's on the ground, pale, not
    moving. His hands are burned at the palms where he gripped
    the wire.

                        GRANT
              Tim! Tim!

    He rolls the boy over. He checks for breathing. There is none.
    He checks for a pulse. There is none.

    Lex drops to her knees.

                        LEX
              Is he — is he —

                        GRANT
              Be quiet!

    He begins CPR. Compressions. He counts under his breath.

    He tilts Tim's head back and breathes into his lungs.

    Compressions again.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              Come on, Tim. Come on.

    Again.

    And again.

    And —

    Tim gasps. His chest heaves. His eyes open — confused,
    terrified, searching.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              That's it. That's it. Stay with me.
              You're okay. You're okay.

    He holds Tim's face in both hands. The kid is looking at him.

                        TIM
              (barely audible)
              ...Did I...

                        GRANT
              You're fine. You're completely fine.

    Lex is crying. She doesn't try to hide it.

    Grant sits back on his heels. He's shaking slightly. He
    doesn't try to hide that either.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant lands and runs to Tim. He's on the ground, pale, not

@graph.synopsis: Muldoon and Ellie move through the jungle toward the
@graph.scriptText:
103 EXT. JUNGLE PATH - DAY

    Muldoon and Ellie move through the jungle toward the
    maintenance shed. They move fast but carefully.

    Muldoon stops at a section of fence. He looks at it.

    The fence has been pushed open at the base. Bent inward.

    Slowly, deliberately.

                        MULDOON
              They got out.

    He stares at the gap. It's exactly the size of a velociraptor
    getting low to the ground.

                        MULDOON (CONT'D)
              Clever girl.

    He turns to Ellie.

                        MULDOON (CONT'D)
              Run. Right to the shed. Don't stop. Don't
              look back. I'll cover you.

    He unlimbers the shotgun. Ellie runs.

    Muldoon watches the jungle on either side of the path.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Muldoon and Ellie move through the jungle toward the

@graph.synopsis: Ellie runs. The shed is ahead — she can see the corrugated
@graph.scriptText:
104 EXT. JUNGLE PATH - CONTINUOUS

    Ellie runs. The shed is ahead — she can see the corrugated
    roof through the trees.

    Behind her: Muldoon is still and watching.

    He sees movement in the jungle to his left.

    He tracks it with the gun. He watches. He knows the thing
    about raptors.

    He's not watching his right side.

    The raptor on the right is very still.

    Muldoon watches the movement to the left. Hm. Suspicious.
    Too obvious.

    He turns right.

    The raptor on the right is there. It's right there. Looking
    at him.

    He almost has time to say it.

                        MULDOON
              Clever —


    [END OF MULDOON]
@graph.contextPackSummary: Ellie runs. The shed is ahead — she can see the corrugated

@graph.synopsis: Ellie makes the shed. She grabs the door handle, pulls it open,
@graph.scriptText:
105 EXT. MAINTENANCE SHED - DAY

    Ellie makes the shed. She grabs the door handle, pulls it open,
    and gets inside.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Ellie makes the shed. She grabs the door handle, pulls it open,

@graph.synopsis: Dark. Smells of oil and machinery. Emergency lighting. Banks
@graph.scriptText:
106 INT. MAINTENANCE SHED - DAY

    Dark. Smells of oil and machinery. Emergency lighting. Banks
    of circuit breakers along the walls.

    Ellie has a flashlight. She sweeps it around.

                        ELLIE
              (into radio)
              I'm in. I'm in the shed. Where are the
              main breakers?

                        HAMMOND (OVER RADIO)
              They're on the south wall. Large panel.
              Red handles. You'll see them.

    She sweeps the light around. South wall. Large panel.

    She crosses to it.

    Then her flashlight hits something on the floor.

    An arm. Just an arm. In a JP staff shirt. Severed.

    ARNOLD'S arm.

    She stares at it.

                        ELLIE
              Oh no. Oh no, Ray —

                        HAMMOND (OVER RADIO)
              Ellie? Are you there?

    She can't speak for a moment.

                        ELLIE
              (steadying herself)
              The main breakers. What am I pulling?

                        HAMMOND (OVER RADIO)
              All the red handles, top to bottom. And
              then the main trip switch on the far right.

    She reaches for the panel. Something moves behind her.

    The raptor comes at her from the shadows.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Dark. Smells of oil and machinery. Emergency lighting. Banks

@graph.synopsis: Ellie RUNS. She's fast. The raptor is faster. It clips her
@graph.scriptText:
107 INT. MAINTENANCE SHED - CONTINUOUS

    Ellie RUNS. She's fast. The raptor is faster. It clips her
    shoulder. She goes down — spins — the raptor's claws slash
    the wall where her head was.

    She's on her feet again. She's at the panel.

    She yanks red handles. One, two, three —

    The raptor comes again. She ducks under it, grabs the main
    trip switch with both hands —
@graph.contextPackSummary: Ellie RUNS. She's fast. The raptor is faster. It clips her

@graph.synopsis: The screens COME ALIVE. Every monitor lighting up. Arnold's
@graph.scriptText:
108 INT. CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

    The screens COME ALIVE. Every monitor lighting up. Arnold's
    console — his board illuminating.

    Hammond stares.

                        HAMMOND
              Systems are coming online!
@graph.contextPackSummary: The screens COME ALIVE. Every monitor lighting up. Arnold's

@graph.synopsis: Ellie gets the door open and gets out. She slams the door
@graph.scriptText:
109 INT. MAINTENANCE SHED - CONTINUOUS

    Ellie gets the door open and gets out. She slams the door
    behind her. The raptor hits the door from inside — hard.

    She runs.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Ellie gets the door open and gets out. She slams the door

@graph.synopsis: She runs, hard, back the way she came. Back toward the main
@graph.scriptText:
110 EXT. MAINTENANCE SHED - CONTINUOUS

    She runs, hard, back the way she came. Back toward the main
    building.

    No Muldoon.

    She runs.
@graph.contextPackSummary: She runs, hard, back the way she came. Back toward the main

@graph.synopsis: Grant and the kids reach the main compound fence — the proper
@graph.scriptText:
111 EXT. FENCE - DAY

    Grant and the kids reach the main compound fence — the proper
    perimeter fence now, with the visitor's center visible through
    the chain-link.

    Grant tries the gate. It's locked. Electronically locked.

                        GRANT
              The power must be back on.

                        TIM
              (looking through the fence)
              I can see the center from here.

    Grant rattles the gate. It won't give.

                        TIM (CONT'D)
              (pointing)
              There's a door on the side. A keypad.

    Grant looks. Tim's right — a maintenance door with an electronic
    keypad.

                        GRANT
              Can you hack that?

                        LEX
              (already looking at it)
              I need to see the keypad.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Grant and the kids reach the main compound fence — the proper

@graph.synopsis: Lex examines the keypad. There's an emergency override — a
@graph.scriptText:
112 EXT. FENCE - CONTINUOUS

    Lex examines the keypad. There's an emergency override — a
    physical backup behind a panel.

    She opens the panel. It's a six-position mechanical code lock.

                        LEX
              I can do this.

    She works it. Grant watches the jungle behind them.

    She gets it on the third try. The door clicks open.

                        TIM
              (genuinely impressed)
              How did you know that?

                        LEX
              I guessed. It's a Honeywell standard default.

    They go through.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Lex examines the keypad. There's an emergency override — a

@graph.synopsis: They cross the compound to the visitor's center entrance.
@graph.scriptText:
113 EXT. VISITOR'S CENTER - DAY

    They cross the compound to the visitor's center entrance.
    Ellie comes around the corner of the building at a dead run
    and almost collides with them.

                        ELLIE
              Alan!

    She grabs him. He grabs back.

                        ELLIE (CONT'D)
              (looking at the kids)
              Oh thank God. Oh thank God.

    She hugs them both, briefly. Then she pulls back.

                        ELLIE (CONT'D)
              Muldoon's gone. Ray's gone. We have to
              get inside and get to the control room.
              The phones —

                        GRANT
              The phones may be back on.

                        ELLIE
              The raptors are loose. In the compound.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They cross the compound to the visitor's center entrance.

@graph.synopsis: They look at the building's entrance.
@graph.scriptText:
114 EXT. VISITOR'S CENTER ENTRANCE - CONTINUOUS

    They look at the building's entrance.

                        GRANT
              We go. Together. Fast. Don't stop for
              anything.

    They go.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They look at the building's entrance.

@graph.synopsis: The building is quiet. Emergency lighting still, but the
@graph.scriptText:
115 INT. VISITOR'S CENTER - DAY

    The building is quiet. Emergency lighting still, but the
    main power is cycling back on. Lights are flickering to life.

    They move through the main hall. The dinosaur skeleton above
    them casts complex shadows.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The building is quiet. Emergency lighting still, but the

@graph.synopsis: They pass through the restaurant.
@graph.scriptText:
116 INT. VISITOR'S CENTER RESTAURANT - DAY

    They pass through the restaurant.

    HAMMOND is still there. He looks up — and relief floods his
    face at the sight of them.

                        HAMMOND
              You're alive.

                        GRANT
              Where are the phones?

                        HAMMOND
              The system should be coming back up. The
              control room —

                        GRANT
              Come on.

    They keep moving.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They pass through the restaurant.

@graph.synopsis: They move down the corridor toward the control room.
@graph.scriptText:
117 INT. VISITOR'S CENTER - CORRIDOR - DAY

    They move down the corridor toward the control room.

    Behind them: a sound. A click. The sound of something large
    moving in the restaurant they just left.

    Nobody stops to check.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They move down the corridor toward the control room.

@graph.synopsis: They emerge into a larger gallery area. Through the windows:
@graph.scriptText:
118 INT. VISITOR'S CENTER - DAY

    They emerge into a larger gallery area. Through the windows:
    the park outside. The brachiosaurus herd, moving slowly.
    Peaceful.

                        TIM
              (sotto voce, walking)
              They don't even know.

    A figure moves in the shadows at the far end of the gallery.

    They stop.

    One of the raptors is in the building.

    It's watching them.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They emerge into a larger gallery area. Through the windows:

@graph.synopsis: They back into the kitchen. Steel surfaces, gas ranges, hanging
@graph.scriptText:
119 INT. VISITOR'S CENTER KITCHEN - DAY

    They back into the kitchen. Steel surfaces, gas ranges, hanging
    pots. A large commercial space.

    Grant pushes the kids behind a prep counter.

                        GRANT
              (barely a whisper)
              Lex, Tim. Under there. Don't come out.
              Whatever happens.

                        TIM
              (equally quiet)
              There's two of them.

    He's right. A second raptor has appeared at the kitchen door.

    They're hunting. Methodically. Sweeping the room with their
    eyes.

    Lex and Tim disappear under the counter. Grant and Ellie
    press flat to the wall.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They back into the kitchen. Steel surfaces, gas ranges, hanging

@graph.synopsis: The first raptor enters. It moves slowly, head low, scanning.
@graph.scriptText:
120 INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS

    The first raptor enters. It moves slowly, head low, scanning.
    Its claws tick against the tile floor.

    Lex, under the counter, can see its feet. She stares at them.

    On the counter above Lex: a JELL-O dessert in a dinosaur
    egg cup — from the food display, untouched. The raptor's
    footfalls make the Jell-O quiver. It trembles in perfect
    rhythm with the raptor's movements.

    Lex watches the Jell-O.

    The raptor passes the counter.

    It's moving toward the far side of the kitchen.

    Tim, from his position, can see the second raptor — still
    in the doorway. Watching. Waiting.

    Tim inches forward. There's a POT on the floor nearby —
    fallen from a rack. He reaches for it slowly, slowly —

    He knocks it. The crash is enormous in the quiet kitchen.

    Both raptors react.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The first raptor enters. It moves slowly, head low, scanning.

@graph.synopsis: The raptors orient on the sound. Tim is up and running.
@graph.scriptText:
121 INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS

    The raptors orient on the sound. Tim is up and running.
    Grant launches himself across the kitchen to draw their
    attention.

                        GRANT
              HEY!

    The first raptor goes for Grant. He ducks behind a prep
    station. The raptor goes over the top of it — claws scraping
    steel.

    Ellie grabs a hanging pot and swings it. Hits the second raptor
    across the snout. It recoils — temporarily confused.

    Tim has reached the far walk-in freezer door. He yanks it open.

                        TIM
              Hey! HEY! OVER HERE!

    The first raptor locks on him. He stands his ground in the
    doorway of the freezer.

    One step. Two steps.

    He jumps sideways at the last moment. The raptor's momentum
    carries it into the freezer. He slams the door. The raptor
    throws itself against it from inside — the door shudders —
    Tim leans into it, putting his whole weight against the handle.

                        TIM (CONT'D)
              (strained)
              Lock it! Lock it!

    Lex has appeared from under the counter. She gets her hands
    on the latch and throws the lock.

    The raptor SLAMS into the door. And again. And again.

    Then: quiet.

    Lex and Tim look at each other across the kitchen.

                        LEX
              You're an idiot.

                        TIM
              I know.

    The second raptor is still loose.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The raptors orient on the sound. Tim is up and running.

@graph.synopsis: The group moves fast through the building — Grant, Ellie, and
@graph.scriptText:
122 INT. VISITOR'S CENTER - CORRIDOR - DAY

    The group moves fast through the building — Grant, Ellie, and
    the kids. The loose raptor is somewhere behind them.

    They reach the control room door.

    It's locked. The keypad is active — power's back on.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The group moves fast through the building — Grant, Ellie, and

@graph.synopsis: Inside: empty. Arnold's console, all the monitors alive and
@graph.scriptText:
123 INT. CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

    Inside: empty. Arnold's console, all the monitors alive and
    glowing. The park spread across the screens.

    Ellie gets the door open from a master panel — she knows
    where it is from her earlier visit.

    They pile in. Grant pulls the door shut behind them.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Inside: empty. Arnold's console, all the monitors alive and

@graph.synopsis: Hammond enters from a connecting passage. He reaches for a
@graph.scriptText:
124 INT. CONTROL ROOM - DAY

    Hammond enters from a connecting passage. He reaches for a
    phone on Arnold's console.

                        HAMMOND
              If the phones are back —

    He picks it up. Listens.

                        HAMMOND (CONT'D)
              Dial tone! There's a dial tone!

                        GRANT
              Call the mainland. Get the helicopter.
              Get us out.

    Hammond begins dialing.

    Lex is at Arnold's console. Her eyes are moving over the
    screens, the layout, the interface.

                        LEX
              I know this system.

    She says it with total calm.

                        ELLIE
              What?

                        LEX
              It's a Unix system.

    She sits down at the console.

                        LEX (CONT'D)
              I know this.

    She begins to type.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Hammond enters from a connecting passage. He reaches for a

@graph.synopsis: On the screens: file systems, directory structures. Lex
@graph.scriptText:
125 INT. CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

    On the screens: file systems, directory structures. Lex
    navigates them with practiced ease. Her face is focused,
    intent.

                        TIM
              (watching)
              She's actually doing it.

                        ELLIE
              (watching)
              Lex...

                        LEX
              Don't talk to me.

    She navigates. Finds what she's looking for.

    Across the wall of monitors: a status screen shows door locks
    one by one going ACTIVE. Green lights. The system is coming
    back on.

                        LEX (CONT'D)
              I have the door security system.

                        GRANT
              Can you lock this room?

    As she reaches to do it —

    A crash. The ceiling vent above the door BUCKLES and tears
    loose. A raptor drops into the room.

    Everyone MOVES.
@graph.contextPackSummary: On the screens: file systems, directory structures. Lex

@graph.synopsis: The raptor lands among the consoles. It's in close quarters —
@graph.scriptText:
126 INT. CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

    The raptor lands among the consoles. It's in close quarters —
    too tight for a running hunt.

    Grant grabs a metal chair and swings it, driving the raptor
    back. Ellie gets between the raptor and the kids, arms wide.

                        ELLIE
              Go! NOW! Everyone out!

    The group retreats through the back door — the same way
    Hammond came in. Grant is last, keeping the raptor at bay
    with the chair.

    He gets through. The raptor launches at the door — he pulls
    it shut. Hears the raptor slam into it from the other side.

    They're in the corridor behind the control room.

                        GRANT
              Where does this go?

                        HAMMOND
              The atrium. Then the lobby.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The raptor lands among the consoles. It's in close quarters —

@graph.synopsis: They move through the atrium — a wide space with a lowered
@graph.scriptText:
127 INT. ATRIUM / CRAWL SPACE - DAY

    They move through the atrium — a wide space with a lowered
    ceiling grid. The grid is access panels — the crawl space
    above the ceiling of the whole visitor's center.

    Behind them: the control room door opens. The raptor is through.

    Grant looks up. An access panel.

                        GRANT
              Up. Everyone up.

    He boosts Tim, then Lex. Ellie goes up. Hammond, with some
    help and a great effort. Grant last.

    He gets the panel back in place just as the raptor hits it
    from below.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They move through the atrium — a wide space with a lowered

@graph.synopsis: Above the ceiling. Dark. Dusty. The supports and ducting of
@graph.scriptText:
128 INT. CRAWL SPACE - CONTINUOUS

    Above the ceiling. Dark. Dusty. The supports and ducting of
    the whole building.

    They crawl. It's a labyrinth of pipes and beams. Below them,
    through the ceiling panels, they can hear the raptor moving.

    LEX goes first — she's smallest, fastest.

                        GRANT (WHISPERING)
              Straight ahead. Follow the lights.

    Emergency lighting runs along a central duct. They follow it.

    Below: the raptor moves, tracking by sound. It hits a ceiling
    panel with one claw — the panel flexes down and back up.
    They all freeze.

    Then keep moving.

    LEX reaches an intersection and pauses, looking both ways.

                        GRANT (WHISPERING) (CONT'D)
              Left. The lobby is to the left.

    She goes left. The others follow.

    Behind them: a panel CAVES IN. The raptor is trying to
    come up through the ceiling.
@graph.contextPackSummary: Above the ceiling. Dark. Dusty. The supports and ducting of

@graph.synopsis: Lex is moving fast. Grant is at the back. The raptor's
@graph.scriptText:
129 INT. CRAWL SPACE - CONTINUOUS

    Lex is moving fast. Grant is at the back. The raptor's
    claw punches through a panel behind him — close.

    He keeps moving.

    Lex reaches a main grate — the lobby, below.

                        LEX (WHISPERING)
              Here! I can see it!
@graph.contextPackSummary: Lex is moving fast. Grant is at the back. The raptor's

@graph.synopsis: They drop down through the grate into the main lobby rotunda.
@graph.scriptText:
130 INT. LOBBY / ROTUNDA - DAY

    They drop down through the grate into the main lobby rotunda.
    The two massive dinosaur skeletons arch above them.

    Grant is last. He drops.

    The lobby is dim, high-ceilinged, echoing. Outside the tall
    windows: afternoon sun. Beautiful and completely indifferent.

    They turn.

    Both raptors are in the lobby.

    They've been here. Waiting.

    The group backs against the base of the Brachiosaurus skeleton.

    One raptor goes left. One goes right. They're flanking.

    Grant puts the kids behind him. Ellie next to him.

                        ELLIE
              (very quietly)
              Alan.

                        GRANT
              (equally quiet)
              I know.

    The raptors close in.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They drop down through the grate into the main lobby rotunda.

@graph.synopsis: The raptor on the left leaps.
@graph.scriptText:
131 INT. ROTUNDA - CONTINUOUS

    The raptor on the left leaps.

    A massive impact from the right.

    The T-REX bursts through the far wall of the lobby.

    In a single catastrophic motion she takes the leaping raptor
    in her jaws. The raptor thrashes once. Goes limp.

    The second raptor — confused, outmatched, instinctively
    afraid — backs against the wall.

    The T-Rex shakes the first raptor and throws it aside.

    She turns to the second raptor.

    The second raptor makes a run for the door. The T-Rex is faster.

    The group presses flat against the skeleton base, not moving.
    They are twenty feet away. The T-Rex doesn't see them. Her
    focus is entirely on the second raptor.

    The second raptor is gone.

    The T-Rex stands in the middle of the ruined lobby, chest
    heaving, head high.

    The "WHEN DINOSAURS RULED THE EARTH" promotional banner,
    strung high on the wall, tears loose in the disturbance
    and drifts down. It settles across the T-Rex's back.

    She stands there. Banner draped over her. The light through
    the broken wall falls on her in golden shafts.

                        TIM
              (barely audible)
              ...She saved us.

    The T-Rex opens her jaws and roars. It shakes the building.
    It shakes their chests. It shakes the world.

    Then she looks around. She looks at the group.

    A long moment.

    Then she loses interest. She turns and walks through the
    broken wall and is gone.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The raptor on the left leaps.

@graph.synopsis: They stand there.
@graph.scriptText:
132 INT. LOBBY - CONTINUOUS

    They stand there.

    Nobody moves.

    Then — from outside — the sound of a JEEP. HAMMOND at the
    wheel, arriving in front of the main entrance.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They stand there.

@graph.synopsis: They come out. They're blinking in the afternoon light.
@graph.scriptText:
133 EXT. VISITOR'S CENTER - CONTINUOUS

    They come out. They're blinking in the afternoon light.

    Ellie goes to Hammond. He hugs her. He looks at the kids and
    his face crumples slightly.

                        HAMMOND
              (to the kids)
              I'm sorry. I'm so sorry for all of this.

    Tim reaches out and takes his hand.

                        TIM
              It's okay, Grandpa.

    Hammond closes his eyes.

    MALCOLM is in the back of the jeep, leg still splinted, pale
    and sweating.

                        MALCOLM
              How many made it?

    Nobody answers immediately.

                        GRANT
              Us. Just us.

    Malcolm nods.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They come out. They're blinking in the afternoon light.

@graph.synopsis: A helicopter is landing. The big In-Gen corporate machine
@graph.scriptText:
134 EXT. HELIPAD - DAY

    A helicopter is landing. The big In-Gen corporate machine
    from the mainland, called in. WORKERS run out to meet it.

    The group moves across the compound toward it.

    Grant has a hand on Tim's shoulder. Tim is limping slightly —
    the fence knocked his ankle. Lex has attached herself to
    Ellie's hand.

                        HAMMOND
              (walking beside Grant)
              I'll never build another park. You know that.

                        GRANT
              I know.

    A pause.

                        HAMMOND
              D'you think there could be a park where —
              no. No. I know. I know the answer to that.

    He doesn't finish the thought.
@graph.contextPackSummary: A helicopter is landing. The big In-Gen corporate machine

@graph.synopsis: They board the helicopter. One by one.
@graph.scriptText:
135 EXT. PARK / HELICOPTER APPROACH - DAY

    They board the helicopter. One by one.

    Malcolm is loaded carefully, his leg extended.

    Hammond is last. He stands at the door of the helicopter
    and looks back at the park — at the trees, at the shapes
    moving distantly among them.

    His park. His dream.

    He gets in.

    The helicopter lifts.
@graph.contextPackSummary: They board the helicopter. One by one.

@graph.synopsis: The helicopter banks away from the island. Through the windows:
@graph.scriptText:
136 EXT. AIR - DAY

    The helicopter banks away from the island. Through the windows:
    Isla Nublar, dense with green, the compound a cluster of
    white buildings amid the jungle.

    No sign of the disaster from this height. It looks peaceful.
    It looks beautiful.

    Inside: Ellie leans against Grant. He puts his arm around her.

    Tim is on Grant's other side, beginning to fall asleep despite
    himself. Lex is next to Ellie.

    Hammond stares out the window. His hand rests on the amber
    top of his cane.
@graph.contextPackSummary: The helicopter banks away from the island. Through the windows:

@graph.synopsis: Grant is awake. Tim is fully asleep, his head against Grant's
@graph.scriptText:
137 INT. HELICOPTER - DAY

    Grant is awake. Tim is fully asleep, his head against Grant's
    arm. Lex is asleep on Ellie's shoulder.

    Ellie is watching Grant.

    He notices her watching him.

                        ELLIE
              What are you thinking?

                        GRANT
              (after a moment)
              Dinosaurs and man. Two species separated by
              sixty-five million years of evolution just
              got to sit down for a weekend.

    A pause.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              I'm thinking about what Muldoon said. That
              they remember.

    Another pause.

                        ELLIE
              And?

                        GRANT
              And I'm thinking... I'm thinking about
              the kids.

    She watches him.

                        GRANT (CONT'D)
              I don't hate kids.

    He says it quietly, like a discovery.

    She leans over and kisses him on the cheek.

    He watches the ocean out the window. Below: pelicans in a
    loose formation, keeping pace with the helicopter for a few
    seconds before falling behind.

    He watches them.

137A EXT. HELICOPTER - DAY

    The helicopter moving away from the island.

    HAMMOND looks back. The island is getting smaller.

    He turns away from the window.

    He sits back in his seat and looks at his hands. The amber
    cane across his knees.

    He says nothing.

@graph.scriptText:
139 INT. HELICOPTER - CONTINUOUS

    Ellie asleep now, head on Grant's shoulder.

    Grant awake. The two kids sleeping. Malcolm asleep, pale,
    his leg extended.

    Grant looks around at all of them.

    He looks down at Tim — the boy's dinosaur book has fallen
    open across his lap. A drawing of a velociraptor. Accurate.

    He carefully closes the book and tucks it into Tim's hands.

    He looks out the window.

    The ocean. The sun. The sky.

    He settles back.

    The helicopter over the open Pacific. Small. Getting smaller.

    Ahead: the coast of Costa Rica. Normal, busy, teeming with
    ordinary human life.

    The helicopter grows smaller.

@graph.synopsis: Grant and the ocean.
@graph.scriptText:
141 INT. HELICOPTER - FINAL

    Grant and the ocean.

    Three brown pelicans come alongside the helicopter — riding
    a thermal, effortless. They fly in easy formation for a long
    moment. Their great wings barely move.

    Sixty-five million years of evolution. Unchanged. Perfect.

    Grant watches them fly.

    Something in his face releases.

    He looks at Tim sleeping beside him. Lex. Ellie.

    He looks back at the birds.

    They bank away, riding the wind, and disappear into the
    blue distance.

    The helicopter flies on.
